On May 27, 1937, a massive, incredibly awe-inspiring, and profoundly iconic feat of modern civil engineering was officially achieved in California. This massive, unprecedented naval victory absolutely cemented Japan's status as a formidable global superpower and directly triggered massive, revolutionary unrest against the deeply humiliated Tsarist regime in Russia.

On May 27, 1964, Jawaharlal Nehru, the massive, towering architect of modern India and its absolute, very first Prime Minister, passed away in New Delhi. His massive legacy of fierce secularism, democratic institutionalization, and Non-Aligned foreign policy heavily defined the entire geopolitical and domestic trajectory of 20th-century India.
